item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the bit with Swedish/French/English at the end is what convinced me. The biblical reference is fine, but the collapsible bit is what makes the article (given that you actually bother to translate the other 2 messages and realize they're completely different than the English one).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So…how have I never seen this before? +1

item.title: My humble thoughts
item.content:encoded: Its good, and the end collapsible adds flavor and direction. Perhaps, more can be placed in the stricken areas. A true message, crossed out. Mental teases of what the truth might be. My humble thoughts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Took the liberty of correcting the Swedish text, as it was a royal mess. It's one thing if it's hard to understand because the sentences don't align semantically, that's mad ravings, lunacy. That's good. But this was such a complete word salad that it detracted from the article. I'm not really sure what you wanted to write, but I tried to make the sentences make sense at least within themselves.

Thought it was pretty meh until I noticed the hidden Biblical reference in the bottom. It was cool, sure, but mostly I like how you included that little Easter Egg. You get a +1 for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nä nu vafan. Jag har ingen aning vad dethär ska föreställa, förutom flera pinsamma stavfel.

Det - Detta
svag - svaga
snålla - snälla
utmaningar , - utmaningar,
försäkrar - ger er (?)

Jag är ledsen, fattar inte ett piss. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's more here than meets the eye, but it isn't obvious.

+ Spoilers
- Hide Spoilers

The note reads COMPLETELY DIFFERENTLY in each language.
I ran it through Google Translate and got the following:
Swedish:
I apologize for the trouble I've caused.
It is a lie.

I assure two challenges, because all of you are weak:
1. I will always be a lobster.
2. I am sick - Please help me.

Worship me.

German:
I do not appreciate the problems you brought me.
I continue to learn how to exist.

There are only two things I can guarantee for the future:

1. I will always be a lobster.
2. I will kill you all.

Go to hell.

English:
I am sorry for the trouble that I've caused you.
I'm still getting the hang of this "magic" thing.

Here are my laws:

1. I will always be a hermit crab.
2. I am hungry.

Happy Thanksgiving.

The invisible text just says "Leviticus 11:12" which is basically saying that seafood without fins or scales shall be an abomination unto you, or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, before seeing the hint to highlight the page, it seemed like a minimalist article with a good idea undercut by the final collapsible. Highlighting actually didn't add much besides the Biblical reference; I'd really suggest getting rid of that last collapsible, or else rewording the crossed-out text. It might be worth getting rid of the explanation, though; I think the first collapsible stands well enough on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me, it's because they reflect reality so well. A vast majority of real world industrial catastrophes and major research accidents begin with phrases like "I'm sure it will be fine.", "Ignore that blinking light, it always does that", and "Cost effective"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a novote until I highlighted the entire document, immediate +1 after.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Taxonomic ranks above genus are normally not italicized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm probably wrong, but shouldn't Nephropidae be italicized? Also, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
seriously what is this site's fascination with watching those guys in particular suffer ;A;

Because scientific methodology is "boring" (NOT my opinion on the matter)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OK, so I think I might have gotten this: Is this a lobster which makes people think it's an anomaly a la that bloody air coupler?

In that case, I'll novote. If it's not and it's genuinely some Prometheus Labs thing, downvote with severe prejudice for another bloody lolPrometheus skip.

seriously what is this site's fascination with watching those guys in particular suffer ;A;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure it doesn't alter reality, just information regarding it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this has a lot of potential. I like reality altering things, but I don't think this is where it needs to be just yet. -1 for now.
